articles.title: Four in five landlords unprepared for Renters’ Right Act
articles.summary: Four in five landlords (82%) are concerned about the impact of the Renters’ Rights Act, with one in two (44%) very concerned. Meanwhile, three-quarters (75%) of landlords say regulation has made them less likely to grow their portfolios, and half (49%) are planning to sell or reduce their stake within two months.
articles.ai_summary:
- **Widespread Concerns:** 82% of landlords are unprepared for the Renters’ Rights Act, with nearly half expressing significant anxiety about its impact.  
- **Portfolio Shrinkage:** 75% of landlords feel that increased regulation is deterring portfolio growth, with nearly half planning to sell or reduce their holdings soon.

articles.title: Energy efficiency and EPC ratings become higher focus for tenants
articles.summary: Landlords must consider how to improve EPC ratings to attract tenants, even ahead of the new requirement to upgrade rental properties to a C rating by 2030. It comes as energy efficiency of properties has gained even greater focus as tenants look to save money after the energy price hikes caused by the Iran War.
articles.ai_summary:
- Tenant demand for energy-efficient properties is rising, prompting landlords to enhance EPC ratings ahead of the 2030 mandate for a minimum C rating.  
- Rising energy costs due to geopolitical factors are making energy efficiency a top priority for tenants seeking to reduce their living expenses.

articles.title: Planning reform fails to inspire confidence – Landbay
articles.summary: Fewer than a quarter of the UK’s landlords feel planning reform will positively impact their business.
articles.ai_summary:
- Less than 25% of UK landlords believe proposed planning reforms will benefit their operations.  
- Lack of confidence in planning changes highlights ongoing challenges in the property market.

articles.title: Landlords warned a hidden leak could cost thousands
articles.summary: With more than half (58%) of hidden leaks originating outside a property, landlords must remember to focus on the maintenance of pipework outside as well as inside the property, leak detection specialist Aspect has warned.
articles.ai_summary:
- **Crucial Maintenance Alert:** Landlords are urged to prioritize the upkeep of external pipework, as 58% of hidden leaks occur outside the property.  
- **Costly Consequences:** Failing to address these hidden leaks could lead to thousands of pounds in repair costs for landlords.

articles.title: As RRA deadline looms, 5 top tips for landlords
articles.summary: From 1 May 2026, the Renters’ Rights Act will implement some of the biggest changes to the Private Rented Sector in England and Wales. The Act will aim to balance landlord and tenant rights and will impose new requirements and regulatory oversight on landlords.
articles.ai_summary:
- The Renters' Rights Act, effective from May 1, 2026, will introduce significant reforms to the Private Rented Sector in England and Wales, aiming to enhance tenant protections while balancing landlord rights.  
- Landlords are advised to prepare for upcoming regulatory changes with key tips to ensure compliance and successful adaptation ahead of the deadline.

articles.title: 84% of landlords not ready for RRA – survey
articles.summary: More than eight in ten (84%) of landlords are unprepared for the Renters’ Rights Act go-live on May 1, with 58% at high risk of non-compliance according to new figures.
articles.ai_summary:
- A staggering 84% of UK landlords lack preparation for the Renters’ Rights Act set to take effect on May 1.  
- Over half (58%) of landlords face a high risk of non-compliance with the upcoming regulations.

articles.title: Expansion no longer default response to rising demand as rental growth slows
articles.summary: Landlords are restructuring portfolios and financing in response to changing rental demand, rather than focusing on expansion, with the ONS announcement of a slowing in rental growth showing the need to ensure portfolios are right.
articles.ai_summary:
- Landlords are pivoting from expansion to restructuring their portfolios and financing in response to slowing rental growth, as highlighted by the ONS data.  
- The focus is now on optimizing existing investments to meet shifting rental demand.

articles.title: Landlords blamed for pushing first-time and local buyers out
articles.summary: Landlords and investors have been blamed for pushing first-time buyers and local buyers out of the market, according to a new survey.
articles.ai_summary:
- A recent survey highlights that landlords and investors are driving first-time and local buyers out of the property market.  
- This trend raises concerns about housing accessibility for those looking to buy their first home.

articles.title: Next government must stem landlord flow
articles.summary: Scotland's landlords have called for the next Scottish Government to formally recognise the private rented sector's (PRS) importance with a dedicated strategy.
articles.ai_summary:
- Landlords in Scotland urge the next government to implement a dedicated strategy to acknowledge the significance of the private rented sector (PRS).  
- The call reflects growing concerns over the stability and support needed for the PRS amidst evolving housing challenges.

articles.title: Landlords beware of registration requirement for tax advisor
articles.summary: Conveyancing solicitors and estate agency payroll teams are among those who could fall foul of new mandatory HMRC tax advisor registration requirements from May, facing penalties of up to £10,000.
articles.ai_summary:
- Starting May, new HMRC regulations require tax advisors, including conveyancing solicitors and estate agency payroll teams, to register or risk penalties up to £10,000.  
- Landlords should be aware of these changes to avoid costly fines by ensuring their tax advisors comply with the registration requirement.

articles.title: Licensing doesn’t lead to higher rents – council’s claim
articles.summary: The council is expecting landlords to pay £694 per property without recovering the cost
articles.ai_summary:
- A council claims that licensing fees for landlords, set at £694 per property, won't cause rent increases for tenants.  
- Landlords face financial burdens as the council does not plan to reimburse licensing costs, potentially impacting their profitability.

articles.title: Labour makes it clear – no rent controls
articles.summary: The pledge was made by Matthew Pennycook
articles.ai_summary:
- Labour's Matthew Pennycook announces a firm stance against implementing rent controls.
- The party aims to clarify its housing policy amidst growing concerns over affordability.

articles.title: Authority wants to stop “HMO landlord free for all”
articles.summary: The council is also drawing up detailed HMO guidelines
articles.ai_summary:
- The council is implementing strict guidelines to regulate the growing trend of Houses in Multiple Occupation (HMOs) and curb landlord exploitation.  
- New measures aim to establish order in the HMO sector and protect tenant rights amid increasing concerns over housing standards.

articles.title: Tenants tutored on maximising Renters Rights Act powers
articles.summary: The week-long campaign begins on Monday
articles.ai_summary:
- A new week-long campaign kicks off on Monday, aimed at educating tenants about their rights under the Renters Rights Act.
- The initiative focuses on empowering renters to fully utilize the legal protections available to them.

articles.title: Renters Rights Act provoking spike in S21 evictions
articles.summary: This is exactly what Paul Shamplina predicted
articles.ai_summary:
- The Renters Rights Act is leading to a significant increase in Section 21 evictions, as forecasted by Paul Shamplina.  
- Landlords are reportedly taking action in response to the new legislation, raising concerns for tenant security.

articles.title: Rents rising ahead of Renters Rights Act – new data
articles.summary: The data comes from the lettings agency Hamptons
articles.ai_summary:
- Rents are climbing as landlords anticipate the impending Renters Rights Act, according to new data from lettings agency Hamptons.  
- The surge in rental prices highlights a proactive shift in the market as landlords adapt to upcoming regulatory changes.

articles.title: Renters Rights Act will kill off the hobbyist landlord – opinion
articles.summary: We're just 10 days away from the Renters Rights Act becoming law
articles.ai_summary:
- The upcoming Renters Rights Act, set to become law in just 10 days, is poised to significantly impact amateur landlords in the UK.  
- Critics argue that the legislation will drive many hobbyist landlords out of the market, reshaping the rental landscape.

articles.title: Gen Z entrepreneurs turn to property investment
articles.summary: Despite the red tape and extra tax poured on property investors in recent years.
articles.ai_summary:
- Gen Z entrepreneurs are increasingly diving into property investment, navigating through new taxes and regulations.
- This trend highlights a shift in investment strategies among younger generations, even amid challenging market conditions.

articles.ai_summary:

articles.title: Nine out of ten homes don’t reach the open sales market
articles.summary:
The reality is that many new homes being built will never reach the market, says Riccardo Iannucci-Dawson, Chief Executive of Alto.
The post Nine out of ten homes don’t reach the open sales market appeared first on The Negotiator.
articles.ai_summary:
- A staggering 90% of newly constructed homes never make it to the open sales market, according to Riccardo Iannucci-Dawson, CEO of Alto.  
- This trend highlights significant barriers in property visibility and accessibility for potential buyers.
